page: Google Cloud Platform n4d-standard-32 is a general-purpose virtual machine instance belonging to the n4d server family. It is built on the x86_64 CPU architecture and features 32 dedicated vCPUs paired with 128.0 GB of system memory. This configuration establishes a balanced resource allocation ratio of 4.0 GB of RAM per virtual core.

This instance type does not include local NVMe SSD storage, requiring reliance on network-attached storage options for persistent data. Additionally, the configuration contains zero GPUs, meaning it is not designed for hardware-accelerated graphical or deep learning workloads. No complimentary public IPv4 addresses are bundled with the instance.

The n4d-standard-32 provides a balanced resource density suitable for standard enterprise workloads. Because it features dedicated CPU allocation without the overhead of specialized hardware like GPUs or local SSDs, it offers a predictable cost-to-resource profile for general-purpose computing. Appropriate workloads include web serving, application servers, containerized microservices, and medium-scale database hosting where local disk I/O is not a primary bottleneck.
description: Google Cloud Platform n4d-standard-32 is an x86_64 general-purpose virtual machine instance featuring 32 dedicated vCPUs and 128.0 GB of system memory. This configuration provides a balanced resource ratio of 4.0 GB of RAM per virtual core, making it suitable for standard enterprise applications. The instance does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, which simplifies the hardware profile and focuses resources on core compute and memory capacity. Without bundled local SSDs or public IPv4 addresses, storage and external networking must be configured via network-attached services. The n4d-standard-32 is designed for workloads such as web serving, application hosting, containerized microservices, and general-purpose business applications that require dedicated CPU allocation without specialized hardware acceleration.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n4d-standard-32 is an x86_64 general-purpose instance featuring 32 dedicated vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and no local storage or GPUs, designed for balanced enterprise workloads.

tagline: A balanced x86_64 virtual machine featuring thirty-two dedicated virtual cores and one hundred twenty-eight gigabytes of memory for general-purpose workloads.
bullet_points: 128.0 GB of system memory providing 4.0 GB per core
No integrated GPUs or local SSD storage included
Suited for web serving, application hosting, and containerized microservices
